AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG US HWY 259 JUST SOUTHWEST OF DE KALB TX AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 7 MILES ENDING 2.7 MILES NORTHEAST OF DE KALB TX. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG WITH SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 5 MILES NORTH OF MALTA TX ALONG COUNTY ROAD 3109 WHERE IT TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 0.55 MILES ENDING 5.25 MILES NORTHEAST OF MALTA TX. THE TORNADO DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A CHURCH AND HOME.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 7 MILES SOUTHWEST OF FOREMAN, AR AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 6.5 MILES BEFORE ENDING A FEW MILES SOUTHEAST OF FOREMAN. THE TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE TO SEVERAL STRUCTURES INCLUDING A LARGE BARN AND TWO SEMI TRAILERS BEFORE CROSSING HIGHWAY 41 WHERE SEVERAL TREES AND UTILITY POLES WERE SNAPPED. A CHURCH AND SEVERAL HOMES ALSO SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE BEFORE THE TORNADO FINALLY LIFTED JUST NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD 76.

BASEBALL SIZE HAIL FELL IN SCOTTSVILLE.

*** 1 FATAL, 7 INJ *** A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM TRACKED SOUTHEAST ACROSS LAKE OF THE PINES IN SOUTHWEST MARION COUNTY, PRODUCING A SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE ACROSS THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE LAKE, WITH THE MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRING AT THE BRUSHY CREEK CAMPGROUND. NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED WITH THE 80-90 MPH DOWNBURST. SEVERAL TREES LANDED ON CAMPERS, TENTS, AND VEHICLES. OF THE NEARLY 238 REGISTERED CAMPERS, SEVEN WERE TREATED FOR INJURIES, AND ONE FATALITY OCCURRED, WHEN A TREE LANDED ON A TENT. THEY ALSO HAD GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL AT THE CAMPGROUND. FOR THE RECORD, THIS DAMAGE OCCURRED ON MARCH 10TH AT 11:55 PM CST. HOWEVER, FOR THE PURPOSES OF THIS LSR, WE ARE CONVERTING TO MARCH 11TH 12:55 AM CDT.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY. 133 JUST SOUTH OF HEBERT WHERE IT UPROOTED A TREE. IT CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST WHERE IT PARALLELED HWY. 561; IT UPROOTED OR TWISTED SEVERAL TREES HERE AND UPLIFTED SHINGLES AND THE ROOFING ON TWO MOBILE HOMES. THE TORNADO THEN DESTROYED AN OUT-BUILDING AND A BARN RIGHT BEFORE CROSSING INTO RICHLAND PARISH. PATH LENGTH: 1.07 MILES, PATH WIDTH: 350 YARDS, ESTIMATED PEAK WIND: 95 MPH.

WATER OVER SECONDARY ROADS ACROSS PARISH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY. 71 SOUTH OF CLARENCE, LOUISIANA WHERE IT KNOCKED SOME LARGE BRANCHES DOWN AND TWISTED SOME TOPS OFF OF TREES. IT CONTINUED EAST NORTHEAST WHERE IT PARALLELED HWY. 84 AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES. IT CONTINUED ALONG HWY. 84 AND CROSSED OVER INTO WINN PARISH AND CAUSED CONSIDERABLE TREE DAMAGE IN THE KISATCHIE NATIONAL FOREST BEFORE THE STORM STARTED TO TAKE MORE OF A NORTHEASTERLY TURN NEAR THE SOUTHERN PART OF SALINE LAKE. IT BEGAN TO PARALLEL LAKESHORE DRIVE AND MULLIGAN INN LOOP. SEVERAL HOMES IN THE AREA ALONG THE EAST BANK OF THE LAKE RECEIVED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE FROM TREES FALLING ON THE HOMES; IN THIS AREA THE TORNADO WAS AT ITS STRONGEST AND REACHED ITS GREATEST WIDTH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEASTWARD CAUSING SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE ALONG SALINE LAKE ROAD BEFORE IT LIFTED NEAR THE VICINITY OF COLDWATER ROAD JUST TO THE SOUTHWEST OF CALVIN, LOUISIANA. PATH LENGTH: 16.10 MILES, PATH WIDTH: 1200 YARDS, ESTIMATED PEAK WIND 105 MPH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT SOURCE FROM 7 SSE DOWNSVILLE. AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN JUST NORTH OF THE CALHOUN COMMUNITY AND MOVED NORTH-NORTHEAST WHERE IT SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES. FALLING TREES CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG 837 AND RICHARDSON RD. A BARN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AND A MOBILE HOME WAS FLIPPED ALONG 837 AS WELL...BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.91 MILES...MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 600 YARDS...AND MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 110 MPH.

THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST NORTH OF THE CALHOUN COMMUNITY AND MOVED NORTH-NORTHEAST WHERE IT SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES. FALLING TREES CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG 837 AND RICHARDSON RD. A BARN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AND A MOBILE HOME WAS FLIPPED ALONG 837 AS WELL...BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 107 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF THE COMMUNITY OF CUSHING, IN A FORESTED AREA WEST OF 6TH STREET. THIS TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST ON THE SOUTH AND EAST SIDE OF CUSHING, WHERE IT PICKED UP AND FLIPPED BOTH A TRAMPOLINE AND AN ABOVE GROUND POOL WHILE DAMAGING THE FRAME OF A MOBILE HOME. THE MOST FOCUSED DAMAGE WAS ALONG THE CORNER OF EUCALYPTUS AVENUE AND 4TH STREET, WHERE SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, IT TORE OFF HALF OF A SHEET METAL ROOF OF A HOUSE AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 204, WHILE ALSO SNAPPING A CROSS OF A POWER POLE. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 204 ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF CUSHING. THE ESTIMATED PATH WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 100 YARDS, WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES.

*** 2 INJ *** AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 134 MPH AND A PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS CONTINUED ON THE GROUND FROM NORTHERN RAPIDES PARISH INTO SOUTHEAST GRANT PARISH, WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG CLEAR CREEK ROAD. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST TO DUG LECKIE ROAD, WHERE A MOBILE HOME WITH 2 ADULTS AND 5 CHILDREN WAS DESTROYED. TWO OF THESE FAMILY MEMBERS SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, CROSSING HARDWATER LAKE ROAD WHERE ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, BEFORE CROSSING OVER INTO LA SALLE PARISH.

THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE GRANT PARISH TORNADO. THIS EF-2 TORNADO, WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS NEAR 134 MPH AND A PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS, CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS A HEAVILY WOODED AREA, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES. A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED ON CECIL ROAD, AFTER IT WAS LIFTED, FLIPPED, AND MOVED APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS AS THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST ACROSS HIGHWAYS 8 AND 772. NEAR THIS INTERSECTION, ON MAYO ROAD, A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED WITH 5 OTHER MOBILE HOMES, 3 STRUCTURES, AND 4 FAMILY HOMES SUFFERING PARTIAL DAMAGE. ON HIGHWAY 772, THE ROOF OF A MOBILE HOME WAS PARTIALLY RIPPED OFF. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO THE TROUT COMMUNITY, WHERE ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AND A PORTION OF A ROOF WAS REMOVED ON CHURCH STREET. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF BILTMORE DRIVE. THE TOTAL TORNADO PATH LENGTH FROM RAPIDES PARISH SOUTHWEST OF ALEXANDRIA INTO CENTRAL LA SALLE PARISH WAS APPROXIMATELY 38 MILES.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 8. THIS DAMAGE EXTENDS NORTH NORTHEAST TO JUST SOUTH OF THE SUMMERVILLE COMMUNITY.
TREES DOWN ON A HOME ON HIGHWAY 772 JUST WEST OF JENA, WITH A RESIDENT TRAPPED IN THE HOME.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN A HEAVILY WOODED AREA NORTH OF HIGHWAY 507 NORTHWEST OF BIENVILLE, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ACROSS SHILOH AND BAILEY ROADS. APPROXIMATELY 15 TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THESE TWO ROADS, WITH THE TORNADO CONTINUING NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING PRIOR TO REACHING COLLINGSWORTH ROAD JUST SOUTH SOUTHWEST OF BRYCELAND. THE ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 4.1 MILES, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO MOVED ACROSS RURAL HEMPSTEAD COUNTY DURING THE EARLY MORNING OF DECEMBER 1ST. DAMAGE WAS INTERMITTENT ALONG ITS PATH FROM NEAR SPRING HILL TO 3 MILES EAST OF HOPE. DAMAGE INCLUDED DESTRUCTION TO A TIN ROOF ON A BARN, A DESTROYED CARPORT, COLUMN AND ANCHORAGE FAILURE OF A HEAVY MACHINERY SHELTER, AND MANY UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES.
